title: Optional Properties
description: Handle data that might not exist.

slides.title: Incomplete Data 🧩
slides.content:
Sometimes objects don't have all their data filled out immediately.

For example, some users might provide an `email`, but others might leave it blank.

By default, Interfaces require EVERY single property to be present.

slides.title: The Question Mark ❓
slides.content: Just like optional parameters in functions, we can mark an Interface property as optional using a `?`.

quizData.question: If you access an optional property that the user didn't provide (like `guest.email`), what is its value in JavaScript?

options.text: It throws an error during execution.

quizData.explanation: If an object property exists in the interface but no data is assigned, accessing it returns `undefined`.

slides.title: Handling Undefined 🦺
slides.content: Because TypeScript knows `email` is optional, it will force you to check if the property actually exists before trying to call `.toLowerCase()` on it, saving you from crashes!

slides.title: Safe Checking
slides.content: Here is how you safely handle optional data:
